channel.title: Information Architecture on Mark Breaux | Information Architecture for the AI Era
channel.description: Recent content in Information Architecture on Mark Breaux | Information Architecture for the AI Era

item.title: Evergreen Content Strategy
item.description:
Reduced NI’s product documentation library from 1.1 million pages to 250,000 — a 77% reduction — through a research-driven evergreen content model. The result: improved search findability, stronger SEO, and a consistent documentation experience across a portfolio of nearly 5,000 products.

The Challenge

Over two decades, NI’s documentation had grown without a governing architecture. Each product team created content independently, resulting in a fragmented library of millions of pages with no predictable structure, no consistent naming, and no shared navigation patterns.

item.title: User Manual Standardization
item.description:
How do you design a documentation structure that works for customers across thousands of products? You start by asking how they search. This case study covers the research process that produced the User Manual model now used across NI’s entire product portfolio — and why each structural decision was made the way it was.

The Challenge

NI’s documentation had accumulated over decades without a governing structural standard. Product teams made their own decisions about what documents to create and how to organize them — resulting in an inconsistent landscape of Getting Started Guides, Feature Usage documents, Process docs, and reference material organized in ways that made sense to the teams that created them, but not necessarily to the customers who needed to use them.
